Overview

Address
1415 WEST FOSTER AVENUE, CHICAGO, IL 60640

Setting
Urban
2 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 2/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 3/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 4.13 4.61 3.46 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.82 0.81 0.69 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.87 1.02 0.73 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.44 2.78 2.04 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.69 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.08 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.49
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.69
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
2.93
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
56.6%
annual
RN turnover
42.1%
annual
Administrators departed
1
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.63
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
1.18
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-01-16 25 23 2 208 1 208
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-01-25 14 8 6 120 1 120
Total weighted health score
186.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (45)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

43 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
2 G/H/I Actual harm
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-12-08 2025-12-11
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2025-08-27 2025-08-29
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2025-01-16 2025-02-10
0565 Honor the resident's right to organize and participate in resident/family groups in the facility. F 2025-01-16 2025-02-10
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2025-01-16 2025-02-10
0636 Assess the resident completely in a timely manner when first admitted, and then periodically, at least every 12 months. D 2025-01-16 2025-02-10
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2025-01-16 2025-02-10
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2025-01-16 2025-02-10
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2025-01-16 2025-02-10
0687 Provide appropriate foot care. G 2025-01-16 2025-02-10
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-01-16 2025-02-10
0693 Ensure that feeding tubes are not used unless there is a medical reason and the resident agrees; and provide appropriate care for a resident with a feeding tube. D 2025-01-16 2025-02-10
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2025-01-16 2025-02-10
0725 Provide enough nursing staff every day to meet the needs of every resident; and have a licensed nurse in charge on each shift. D 2025-01-16 2025-02-10
0726 Ensure that nurses and nurse aides have the appropriate competencies to care for every resident in a way that maximizes each resident's well being. F 2025-01-16 2025-02-10
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. E 2025-01-16 2025-02-10
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. D 2025-01-16 2025-02-10
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. D 2025-01-16 2025-02-10
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2025-01-16 2025-02-10
0813 Have a policy regarding use and storage of foods brought to residents by family and other visitors. E 2025-01-16 2025-02-10
0838 Conduct and document a facility-wide assessment to determine what resources are necessary to care for residents competently during both day-to-day operations (including nights and weekends) and emergencies. F 2025-01-16 2025-02-10
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. F 2025-01-16 2025-02-10
0883 Develop and implement policies and procedures for flu and pneumonia vaccinations. E 2025-01-16 2025-02-10
0887 Educate residents and staff on COVID-19 vaccination, offer the COVID-19 vaccine to eligible residents and staff after education, and properly document each resident and staff member's vaccination status. F 2025-01-16 2025-02-10
0921 Make sure that the nursing home area is safe, easy to use, clean and comfortable for residents, staff and the public. F 2025-01-16 2025-02-10
0921 Make sure that the nursing home area is safe, easy to use, clean and comfortable for residents, staff and the public. F 2024-12-11 2024-12-13
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2024-08-19 2024-08-20
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2024-08-19 2024-08-20
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. E 2024-08-19 2024-08-20
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2024-08-19 2024-08-20
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. G 2024-06-21 2024-06-22
0554 Allow residents to self-administer drugs if determined clinically appropriate. E 2024-01-25 2024-02-12
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2024-01-25 2024-02-12
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. E 2024-01-25 2024-02-10
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2024-01-25 2024-02-12
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2024-01-25 2024-02-12

0813 Have a policy regarding use and storage of foods brought to residents by family and other visitors. D 2024-01-25 2024-02-12
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2024-01-25 2024-02-12

Deficiencies 0725 — survey_date 2025-01-16 0725.survey_date
Deficiencies 0725 — tag 0725 0725.tag
Deficiencies 0726 — correction_date 2025-02-10 0726.correction_date
Deficiencies 0726 — description Ensure that nurses and nurse aides have the appropriate competencies to care for every resident in a way that maximizes each resident's well being. 0726.description
Deficiencies 0726 — scope_severity F 0726.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0726 — survey_date 2025-01-16 0726.survey_date
Deficiencies 0726 — tag 0726 0726.tag

Deficiencies 0761 — description Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. 0761.description
